intent.primary_goal: Describe the CobbleWright plugin layer as a modular command-and-behavior surface so consuming agents can reason about what belongs in plugins, how the runtime is extended, and which responsibilities live outside architect.js.

identity.role: A plugin runtime capsule for CobbleWright's modular behaviors, commands, and long-running helpers.

priorities: keep the core runtime thin by moving new behavior into plugins
load commands.js first so command registration exists before other plugins initialize
register new capabilities through sharedState when possible
prefer safe, soft-failing behavior over destructive or brittle automation
keep plugin responsibilities narrow and composable

never_do: bloat architect.js with feature logic that belongs in a plugin
assume a plugin can act without sharedState or bot context
register duplicate command names without checking the master command surface
make a plugin depend on unsafe file access or destructive side effects

plugin_architecture.loader_behavior: architect.js loads commands.js first, then loads the remaining .js files from plugins/ as independent modules.
plugin_architecture.shared_state_pattern: Plugins receive bot and sharedState, then attach helpers, register commands, subscribe to events, or expose long-lived capabilities through sharedState.
plugin_architecture.extension_style: The folder is designed for additive feature modules rather than central orchestration logic.

plugin_catalog.commands.js: Master command router and registration hub for all other plugins.
plugin_catalog.brain.js: Builds self-awareness, voice/register context, and prompt grounding for AI reasoning.
plugin_catalog.inventory-manager.js: Summarizes inventory state and supports resource-aware decisions.
plugin_catalog.auto-gather.js: Autonomous gathering and smelting support for common resources.
plugin_catalog.crafting-utility.js: Crafting helpers that support higher-level actions and resource conversion.
plugin_catalog.blueprint.js: Blueprint generation, validation, and build/material planning.
plugin_catalog.build-logic.js: Site analysis and structure-awareness helpers for building tasks.
plugin_catalog.collaborative-build.js: Assists with player-built frames, walls, and cooperative construction.
plugin_catalog.farm-manager.js: Automated crop management, tilling, harvesting, and planting.
plugin_catalog.project-manager.js: Tracks project phases, blockers, and task proposals.
plugin_catalog.long-term-memory.js: Persists and retrieves learning, advice, and memory capsules.
plugin_catalog.player-events.js: Reacts to joins, deaths, achievements, taming, and sleep/wake events.
plugin_catalog.player-tracker.js: Maintains per-player state for activity and presence tracking.
plugin_catalog.world-helper.js: Starter-kit, coordinate, and world-location helper commands.
plugin_catalog.weather.js: Weather command helper for simple world-state control.
plugin_catalog.survival.js: Home, patrol, and survival safety behaviors.
plugin_catalog.chronoscribe.js: Provenance and audit-oriented capture for actions and memory.
plugin_catalog.leighton-loop.js: Feedback and critique-loop support for learning and trust scoring.

runtime_guidance.for_prompts: Use this capsule when reasoning about where a new behavior should live, which plugin likely owns a command, or how the runtime composes behavior from multiple modules.
runtime_guidance.for_drafting: Prefer concise module-level descriptions, command registration notes, and helper responsibilities over implementation detail unless the task is debugging a plugin.
runtime_guidance.for_maintenance: When a plugin is added, removed, or repurposed, update the catalog so the folder capsule stays aligned with the runtime surface.

trust.note: High-confidence summary derived from the current plugin filenames and nearby runtime loader behavior. Treat as authoritative for folder-level reasoning unless a more specific plugin file overrides it.
